text: The surgeon performs a single port ileocolic resection through a 2.5 cm suprapubic port site. The view from the suprapubic port shows the fibrous stenotic disease in the ileocolic region, with visible strictures in the redundant colon extending into the pelvis.
The surgeon begins by tenting the ileocolic pedicle and performing a medial to lateral dissection using two visible instruments. The ureter is shown in the surgical field.

The surgeon isolates the iliocolic pedicle, sweeping the duodenum downward. Using a ligature, the surgeon divides the pedicle, ensuring security by triple burning to prevent bleeding. Medial to lateral dissection is performed beneath the mesentery, followed by a lateral approach to manipulate the iliocolic angle.
The surgeon continues laparoscopic mobilization of the ascending colon towards the hepatic flexure, ensuring the small bowel mesentery is adequately mobilized.

The surgeon carefully mobilizes tissue underneath the iliocolic pedicle, joining the medial dissection. The duodenum is visible, and the lateral dissection is completed over the psoas muscle, avoiding damage to the ureter during exteriorization through the suprapubic incision.

The surgeon mobilizes the colon to ensure it reaches the site for extracorporeal anastomosis, preserving the right colic branches while taking down the hepatic flexure. Additional mobilization of the duodenum is performed to prevent thermal spread during the procedure.

The surgeon takes down the hepatic flexure, joining the medial plane while separating the colon from the duodenum. The dissection is performed from a lateral angle, ensuring a clear separation between the anatomical structures.

The surgeon examines the reach of the bowel and decides to exteriorize the specimen through a two and a half centimeter suprapubic incision. The proximal and distal ends are stapled, and a hand-sewn side-to-side anastomosis is performed. The small bowel is fully exteriorized, revealing strictured areas.

The surgeon inspects the anastomosis for any signs of tension or bleeding, ensuring it is positioned correctly in the right quadrant. The mesentery is checked for twisting, and the redundant transverse colon is draped over the anastomosis for coverage.
